sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.77 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Elements$a,b$of a group$G$are said to be symmetric with respect to an element\/$g G$if$a=gb^{-1}g$. Let$G$be an infinite abelian group,$$a cardinal,$<|G|$. Is it true that for any$n$-colour\-ing$: G \{ 0,\,1, ,n-1\}$there exists a monochrome subset of cardinality$$that is symmetric with respect to some element of$G$? This is known to be true for$n 3$.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
